target.name: periplanone B
target.motivation: Periplanone B is a sex pheromone of the American cockroach Periplaneta americana. The paper aims to disclose a formal chemoenzymatic synthesis of this pheromone by accessing a late-stage intermediate.

success_criteria.free_text: Access to cyclodecanone derivative 29, a known late-stage intermediate in the total synthesis of periplanone B.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-C bond formation to form cyclodecanone ring
key_disconnections.reasoning: The key intermediate, cyclodecanone derivative 29, is formed from 'keto'-FPP (15) via enzymatic cyclization catalyzed by Cop4. This leverages the substrate promiscuity of terpene synthases to create complex ring systems.

The initial step in terpene biosynthesis is the enzymatic cleavage of the pyrophosphate moiety from farnesyl pyrophosphate (FPP) derivatives, generating a carbocation that initiates the cyclization cascade.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The strategy focuses on a chemoenzymatic approach, utilizing modified farnesyl pyrophosphate (FPP) derivatives as substrates for sesquiterpene synthases (STSs). The central idea is that STSs, known for their substrate promiscuity, can process unnatural FPP analogues to generate novel terpenoid scaffolds. Specifically, 'keto'-FPP (15) was designed to introduce a ketone functionality that could participate in the cyclization cascade, leading to new oxaterpenoids or, as in the case of 29, a cyclodecanone intermediate relevant to periplanone B synthesis. This approach aims to expand the 'terpenome' and provide access to complex natural product intermediates through biocatalysis.

thought.background: Terpene synthases (TSs) are known to catalyze complex cationic cascade reactions with farnesyl pyrophosphate (FPP) to produce diverse terpenoids. Some STSs exhibit substrate promiscuity, accepting unnatural linear isoprene precursors.
thought.gap: The ability of STSs to process FPP derivatives modified at the central alkene unit, particularly with a keto group, and the resulting structural diversity of products, is not fully explored. The potential for these modified FPPs to yield intermediates for complex natural products is also of interest.
thought.decision: To explore the substrate promiscuity of various STSs with FPP derivatives modified at the central alkene unit, specifically 'keto'-FPP (15), 'iso'-FPP (16), and 'nor-iso'-FPP (17), expecting to generate new terpenoids and potentially access intermediates for known natural products.

outcome.observation: Previous work by the authors and others has shown that STSs can utilize noncanonical FPP derivatives, leading to new sesquiterpene scaffolds. This supports the hypothesis that further modifications could yield novel structures.

evidence.text_span: It has been documented that structural changes around the central olefinic double bond of FPP can provide new sesquiterpene scaffolds.16,22,27 This is particularly relevant for STSs that yield tricyclic products, requiring participation of the central olefinic double bond around C7 in the cation cascade. In the present work, we demonstrate that Omp7 is a highly promiscuous STS that enables a considerable expansion of the “terpenome”. Consequently, we therefore synthesized FPP derivatives 15−17. 22,27 To investigate the catalytic prowess of other members of the STS family of enzymes, we produced four additional enzymes: a caryolanol synthase GCoA ((+)-caryolan-1-ol (18)),28 a viridoflorene/vetispiradiene synthase Tps32 (vetispiradiene (19)),29,30 a β-cubebol synthase Δ (Cop4 (β-cubebol (20)),31 and a patchoulol synthase (Pts patchoulol (21)).32 In addition, we added three more STSs, namely, Jungermania exsertifolia C10-(S)- bicyclogermacrene synthase (JeSTS4 (ent-viridiflorol (22)),33 red macroalgae Laurencia pacifica prespatane synthase (LphTPS prespatane (23)),34 and Picea abies longifolene synthase (PaTPS (longifolene (24))35 to the list of STSs. Noteworthily, these have not been studied in biotransformation with unnatural FPP derivatives so far. The syntheses of FPP derivatives 15−17 have been reported elsewhere,11,12,27 and the final steps in the preparation of the “keto”-FPP analogue 15 are covered in detail in the Supporting Information. Overall, our efforts yielded 18 new terpenoids, including a large variety of new oxaterpenoids and a late stage intermediate in the total synthesis of the American cockroach Periplaneta americana sex pheromone periplanone B.

thought.background: The 'keto'-FPP analogue 15 has been synthesized and is available. Various STSs have been produced and purified.
thought.gap: It is unknown which STSs will successfully convert 'keto'-FPP 15 and what products will be formed.
thought.decision: To perform preparative biotransformations of 'keto'-FPP 15 with seven different STSs (Omp7, GCoA, Tps32, Cop4, Pts, JeSTS4, LphTPS, PaTPS) to identify active enzymes and isolate new products.

outcome.observation: Preparative transformation of ketone 15 yielded sufficient quantities of new products with seven STSs. Omp7 yielded cyclododecanone 25 and six more sesquiterpenoids (26a,b, 27a,b, 28a,b). Cop4 generated cyclodecanone derivative 29. GCoA gave 4-dihydro-2H-pyran terpenoid 30 and hydroxyketone 31. LphTPS and JeSTS4 formed terpenoid 32. PaTPS also gave dihydropyran 30.

evidence.text_span: The preparative transformation of ketone 15 yielded sufficient quantities of new products with seven STSs investigated in this work (Scheme 2A). Only the conversion with Tps32 resulted in a complex mixture; therefore, the use of this STS was not pursued further. Omp7 yielded a bouquet of products, namely, cyclododecanone 25 and six more sesquiterpenoids that can be split into two subgroups. The first group consists of the tertiary alcohols 26a,b and 27a,b, whereby the hydroxyl group localized at the interface of the two rings originates from the keto functionality. The two diastereomeric terpenoids 26a and 26b differ in the localization of one of the two olefins and are syn-annulated. The third terpene alcohol 27b corresponds constitutionally to the isomer 26b, but here, the decahydronaphthalene backbone is anti-fused as in the fourth product of the series, namely, diol 27a. The second subgroup is characterized by an additional ether bridge, which originates from the keto function, too. As such, the decahydronaphthalene backbone is also present in sesquiterpenoids 28a and 28b. Cop4 was found to generate cyclodecanone derivative 29, while GCoA gave 4-dihydro-2H-pyran terpenoid 30 along hydroxyketone 31. Interestingly, we also found this open-chain product in the reaction with PaTPS, which likely gave dihydropyran 30 only under the thermal conditions of GC analysis. The two STSs LphTPS and JeSTS4 are also able to convert FPP ketone 15, whereby in both cases the terpenoid 32 was formed, which is characterized by a bicyclo[8.1.0]- undecan-4-one scaffold. Its constitution and stereochemistry were unequivocally assigned by X-ray analysis of 32. 36a

thought.background: Preparative biotransformations of 'keto'-FPP 15 were performed with various STSs.
thought.gap: Tps32's conversion of 'keto'-FPP 15 resulted in a complex mixture, making product isolation and characterization difficult.
thought.decision: To discontinue the use of Tps32 for 'keto'-FPP 15 due to the complex product mixture, as it hinders further investigation and isolation of specific compounds.

outcome.observation: The conversion of 'keto'-FPP 15 with Tps32 resulted in a complex mixture, making it unsuitable for further study.

evidence.text_span: Only the conversion with Tps32 resulted in a complex mixture; therefore, the use of this STS was not pursued further.

thought.background: Cyclodecanone derivative 29 was isolated from the biotransformation of 'keto'-FPP 15 with Cop4.
thought.gap: The structure of 29 needs to be fully elucidated and confirmed.
thought.decision: To characterize the structure of 29 using NMR spectroscopy, focusing on the 1,3-diene moiety and isopropyl group, and to consider mechanistic implications for its stereochemistry.

outcome.observation: The 1,3-diene moiety in 29 was identified by 1H-1H-COSY spectra. Doublets in the 1H NMR spectrum indicated an isopropyl group. The proposed stereochemistry was based on mechanistic considerations and a large 3J coupling for olefinic protons in a trans-vicinal orientation.

evidence.text_span: The 1,3-diene moiety in 29 was unraveled by 1H-1H-COSY spectra that linked the four olefinic signals at δ = 4.85, 4.82, 5.25, and 5.90 ppm. Doublets in the 1H NMR spectrum for the methyl groups at δ = 0.80 and δ = 0.78 ppm indicate the presence of an isopropyl group. The proposed stereochemistry is based on mechanistic considerations with reference to the transformation of FPP promoted by STS Cop4. No correlation between δ = 5.25 and δ = 5.90 ppm was observed in the 1H-1H-NOESY spectrum. This is supported by a large 3J coupling of 15.8 Hz as expected for olefinic protons in a trans-vicinal orientation.

thought.background: The 'iso'-FPP analogue 16 has been synthesized. Omp7, Pts, JeSTS4, and Tps32 have shown activity with other FPP derivatives.
thought.gap: It is unknown what products will be formed from 'iso'-FPP 16 with these STSs and if any will be structurally novel or relevant.
thought.decision: To perform biotransformations of 'iso'-FPP 16 with Omp7, Pts, JeSTS4, and Tps32 to identify new terpenoids.

outcome.observation: Omp7 yielded three new terpenoids (33−35). Pts yielded bridged bicyclic tertiary alcohol 37. JeSTS4 provided cyclopropyl terpenoid 38 as the main product. Tps32 transformed 16 into valencene (39) as the main product.

evidence.text_span: Omp7, Pts, JeSTS4, and Tps32 proved to be particularly suitable for the transformation of the FPP analogue “iso”-FPP 16 (Scheme 2B). Tree new terpenoids 33−35 were isolated from reactions with Omp7 (Figure 2). To facilitate structure elucidation of bridged sesquiterpenoid 35, we epoxidized it chemically to give derivative 36 and collected and compared a second set of NMR data (see below). Biotransformation of 16 with Pts yielded the bridged bicyclic tertiary alcohol 37, while JeSTS4 provided cyclopropyl terpenoid 38 as the main product. Tps32 transformed 16 into valencene (39) as the main product, which is a known aroma component of citrus fruit. In fact, valencene is a minor product in the in vitro transformation of FPP (1) by the sesquiterpene synthase Tps32, while with “iso”- FPP 16, the product ratio of vetispiradiene (19) and valencene (39) is completely reversed (Figure 2).

thought.background: The 'nor-iso'-FPP analogue 17 has been synthesized. Omp7 has shown broad substrate promiscuity.
thought.gap: It is unknown if Omp7 can process 'nor-iso'-FPP 17 and what products will be formed.
thought.decision: To perform biotransformation of 'nor-iso'-FPP 17 with Omp7 to identify new terpenoids.

outcome.observation: 'Nor-iso' FPP analogue 17 was a viable substrate for Omp7, yielding nor-'germacrene F' (40) and cyclohexane derivative 41 as the main products.
'Nor-iso' FPP analogue 17 was a viable substrate for Omp7, yielding nor-'germacrene F' (40) and cyclohexane derivative 41 as the main products.

evidence.text_span: “Nor-iso” FPP analogue 17 was a viable substrate for Omp7 only with nor-"germacrene F" (40) and cyclohexane derivative 41 as the main products (Scheme 2C).

thought.background: Products 33, 34, 35, 37, 38, 40, and 41 were isolated from biotransformations with 'iso'-FPP 16 and 'nor-iso'-FPP 17.
thought.gap: The structures of these new terpenoids need to be fully elucidated.
thought.decision: To perform detailed NMR analysis (1H, 13C, COSY, HMBC, NOESY) for each new product to determine their constitution and relative stereochemistry. For 35, chemical epoxidation to 36 will provide additional NMR data for confirmation.

outcome.observation: Structures of 33, 34, 35, 37, 38, 40, and 41 were elucidated using various NMR techniques. For 35, epoxidation to 36 provided a second set of NMR data, confirming the structure. X-ray analysis was used for 32.

evidence.text_span: The constitution of 33 was found to be the bicyclic double bond isomer of literature known asteriscadiene (45) (Figure 4).42 In particular, the 1H and 13C NMR signals associated with the atoms of the cyclopentene subunit are almost identical for compounds 33 and 45. We suggest that sesquiterpenoid 34 has the same constitution as a recently published sesquiterpene. However, in 34, the methylene group is syn-orientated to the vicinal CH group as was supported from NOESY correlations of the vicinal Me groups compared to the diastereomer 46 that we reported before.35 Structure elucidation of 35 exerted some challenges due to the presence of four quaternary carbon atoms and a methylene moiety, which is surrounded by two of these quaternary centers. To gain a second set of NMR data, epoxide 36 was prepared as a single diastereomer. The 1H-13C-HMBC, 1H-1H-COSY, and 1H-1H-NOESY correlations for δHb = 3.02 ppm and δHb′ = 1.60 ppm turned out to be diagnostic as these signals were also found in the corresponding NMR spectra of epoxide 36. An 1H-13C ADEQUATE experiment of the epoxide confirmed that the methylene bridge is located between a tertiary alcohol and the oxirane. Structure elucidation of 37 first defined the NMR signals of the isopropylidene group and its neighboring methylene group (δ = 1.86 ppm,). 1H-1H COSY and 1H-13C HMBC correlations indicate the presence of a cyclodecane core bridged by a methylene group (δ = 1.89 and 0.71 ppm). The 13C signal at δ = 72.0 ppm points to a tertiary alcohol. The suggested anti-orientation of the CH group (δ = 1.86 ppm) and the methylene bridge was based on the key 1F-1H NOESY correlation of one proton in the cyclodecane core at δ = 1.34 ppm with both the methylene bridge and the methyl group (δ = 1.67 ppm,) of the isopropylidene group. NMR analysis of product 38 revealed the presence of a cyclopropane ring that is part of the bicyclo[8.1.0]undecane backbone. Especially, the 1H and 13C NMR chemical shifts δ of the cyclopropane protons and carbon atoms (δ = 0.50 ppm/δ = 29.6 ppm and δ = 1.31 ppm/δ = 26.4 ppm) and their proximity to the two geminal methyl groups as judged from the ΔH-Σ13C HMBC spectrum are diagnostic. Cyclodeca-1,6-diene derivative 40 is the nor-derivative of the recently reported "germacrene F" (14).16 The 1H NMR spectrum reveals three alkenes with five overall olefinic protons. Two alkene moieties were found to be part of the macrocyclus while the third alkene unit is 1,1-disubstituted. 1H-1H-NOESY analysis provided information on the orientation of the CH group (δ = 2.08 ppm), and both olefinic protons in 1,3 position/γ-position (ΦλΦ=Φλ13 and 5.24 ppm) are orientated on the same side, while the allylic methyl group (δ = 1.51 ppm,) and the olefinic proton in 1,4 distance (δ = 5.25 ppm,) to the tertiary CH group (δ = 2.08 ppm.) are located on the opposite side of the ring system (see Scheme 2C). Structure elucidation of the cyclohexane derivate 41 relied on the 1H NMR signals of the monosubstituted alkene (δ = 6.10, 5.10, and 5.00 ppm) moiety along with those that relate to the disubstituted alkene of the side chain (δ.δ = 5.59 and 5.50 ppm). These chains are located in vicinal positions at the cyclohexane ring, as was judged from the 1H-1H COSY and 1H-13C HMBC spectra. The relative stereochemistry was determined by 1H-1H NOESY correlations of the cyclohexane protons with either the methyl or the vinyl substituent. More detailed descriptions of the structural elucidations can be found in the Supporting Information.

thought.background: Omp7 is a highly promiscuous STS. Its crystal structure is unknown, but AlphaFold can predict it.
thought.gap: How to alter the product spectrum of Omp7 to generate even more diverse terpenoids.
thought.decision: To use an AlphaFold prediction of Omp7's structure to identify potential mutation sites (F81W, Y172F, Y227F) in the active site, expecting these mutations to alter the enzyme's catalytic activity and product profile with FPP (1) and modified FPPs (15-17).

outcome.observation: AlphaFold prediction suggested three mutations (F81W, Y172F, Y227F) in the active site of Omp7.

evidence.text_span: In an effort to alter the product spectrum of our most versatile STS, Omp7, we sought to determine its crystal structure to enable precision protein engineering. Omp7 proved recalcitrant to crystallization, and we therefore resorted to an AlphaFold prediction of its structure.36b Such models are notoriously unreliable for terpene synthases, and we were thus very careful in our analysis of the model, which suggested three mutations of hydrophobic residues in the active-site of the enzyme (F81W, Y172F, and Y227F).

thought.background: Omp7 mutant F81W was generated based on AlphaFold predictions.
thought.gap: It is unknown how this mutant will affect the product spectrum when incubated with natural FPP (1).
thought.decision: To incubate Omp7 mutant F81W with FPP (1) and analyze the products by GC and NMR after isolation.

outcome.observation: Omp7 mutant F81W produced several new products: sterpurene (42), pentalenene (43), and 7β-protoilludanol (44), in addition to Δ6-protoilludene (2).

evidence.text_span: Omp7 mutant F81W provided several new products when incubated with FPP (1). We were able to detect the formation of sterpurene (42),37 pentalenene (43,)38,39 and 7β-protoilludanol (44)40 initially by GC analysis and spectroscopically proved it by NMR after isolation (Scheme 3A−C).

thought.background: GC analysis of products from Omp7 F81W with FPP (1) showed new sesquiterpenes 42 and 43.
thought.gap: Analytical separation of sterpurene (42) and pentalenene (43) was challenging with standard GC columns.
thought.decision: To try different GC column materials to achieve analytical separation of 42 and 43.

outcome.observation: DB_SHT and ZB 1 columns were not suitable for separating sterpurene (42) and pentalenene (43).
DB_SHT and ZB 1 columns were not suitable for separating sterpurene (42) and pentalenene (43).

evidence.text_span: The choice of the GC column material was crucial in order to achieve the analytical separation of sesquiterpenes 42 and 43. DB_SHT and ZB 1 columns were not suitable; however, an Optima WAX column allowed one to separate sterpurene (42) and pentalenene (43) for analytical GC measurements (for details see SI).

thought.background: Analytical separation of sterpurene (42) and pentalenene (43) was not achieved with DB_SHT and ZB 1 columns.
thought.gap: A suitable GC column is needed for analytical separation.
thought.decision: To use an Optima WAX column for analytical GC measurements to separate sterpurene (42) and pentalenene (43).

outcome.observation: An Optima WAX column successfully separated sterpurene (42) and pentalenene (43) for analytical GC measurements.

evidence.text_span: The choice of the GC column material was crucial in order to achieve the analytical separation of sesquiterpenes 42 and 43. DB_SHT and ZB 1 columns were not suitable; however, an Optima WAX column allowed one to separate sterpurene (42) and pentalenene (43) for analytical GC measurements (for details see SI).

thought.background: Omp7 mutants Y172F and Y227F were generated based on AlphaFold predictions.
thought.gap: It is unknown how these mutants will affect the product spectrum when incubated with natural FPP (1).
thought.decision: To incubate Omp7 mutants Y172F and Y227F with FPP (1) and analyze the products by GC.

outcome.observation: The Omp7 mutants Y172F and Y227F had no significant effect on the product spectrum compared to the wild type (wt) Omp7 with FPP (1).

evidence.text_span: The other two mutations, Y172F and Y227F, had no significant effect on the product spectrum compared to the wild type (wt).

thought.background: Omp7 mutants (F81W, Y172F, Y227F) were generated and tested with FPP (1).
thought.gap: It is unknown how these mutants will perform with the modified FPP derivatives 15-17.
thought.decision: To test the selected Omp7 mutants with substrates 15-17 to see if they convert them and if the product patterns are altered.

outcome.observation: All selected Omp7 mutants were able to convert substrates 15-17. Relative changes in product ratios were found only for 15, with no additional products compared to wt Omp7. Substrates 16 and 17 showed similar product patterns to the wild type.

evidence.text_span: The selected Omp7 mutants all have the ability to convert substrates 15−17. However, according to GC analysis, relative changes in the product ratios were only found for 15, with no additional products found for those produced by wt Omp7. Substrates 16 and 17 generally showed similar product patterns compared to the wild type (see SI).

thought.background: Cyclodecanone derivative 29 was identified as a product from 'keto'-FPP 15 with Cop4.
thought.gap: The relevance of 29 to natural product synthesis, specifically periplanone B, needs to be established.
thought.decision: To highlight the formation of 29 as a formal chemoenzymatic synthesis of a late-stage intermediate for periplanone B, referencing prior chemical syntheses.

outcome.observation: The cyclodecanone derivative 29 is a known late-stage intermediate in the total synthesis of (-)-periplanone B (78). This demonstrates a formal chemoenzymatic synthesis of the pheromone.
The cyclodecanone derivative 29 is a known late-stage intermediate in the total synthesis of (-)-periplanone B (78). This demonstrates a formal chemoenzymatic synthesis of the pheromone.

evidence.text_span: Special attention should be paid to cyclodecanone derivative 29. In the past, it served as the common late stage intermediate in several total syntheses50 directed toward the sex pheromone (−)-periplanone B (78).51 This oxygenated sesquiterpenoid and the reduced derivatives 79 and 80 are excreted by the female American cockroach Periplaneta americana (Scheme 8). In fact, our chemoenzymatic approach to this cyclodecanone demonstrates the potential of using terpene synthases in combination with synthetic chemistry to access advanced intermediates in natural product synthesis. It paves the way for more sophisticated chemosynthetic approaches in the field of terpene synthesis.

Overall Yield.interpretation: Yield for the conversion of 29 to periplanone B in prior chemical syntheses.

validation.comparison_to_prior: The chemoenzymatic synthesis of cyclodecanone derivative 29 provides a new route to a known late-stage intermediate for periplanone B, complementing existing chemical total syntheses.
validation.significance: This work demonstrates the power of chemoenzymatic synthesis to expand the 'terpenome' by utilizing modified FPP derivatives as substrates for promiscuous terpene synthases. It provides access to 18 new terpenoids, including oxaterpenoids, and formally synthesizes a key intermediate for the sex pheromone periplanone B. The study also highlights the potential of site-specific mutagenesis in altering product spectra of STSs.

source.title: Chemoenzymatic Synthesis of Structurally Diverse Terpenoids from Farnesyl Pyrophosphates Modified at the Central Alkene Unit.
source.authors: Struwe H, Slotman C, Höft L, Dräger G, Droste J, Fohrer J, Hausmann K, Beutel S, Kolling D, Köhnke J, Kirschning A.
